entries.senses: Vision favours dappled light and shimmer on water — good contrast sensitivity over long-distance hawk acuity. Hearing is strong for litter rustle and small surface ripples. Chemoreception (smell/taste) leans humid-air and decay-organics, supported by generous nasal turbinate area rather than an external muzzle. Webbing is thermally sensitive for water current and temperature.
entries.anatomy: Beyond baseline human: (1) webbing and thicker palmar/plantar skin; (2) extra lumbar flex for crawling under brush; (3) rib cage modestly broad for comfortable breath-hold in quiet water (seconds to a short minute, not deep diving); (4) slightly richer subcutaneous hydration look in large art — smooth limb reads at 32px rather than ropey tendons. No gills; no powered swimming tail.
entries.spriteNote: At 32px: feet two pixels wide when planted; one low brow mass or a two-pixel eye band; single cheek or teardrop highlight for damp skin. If hands show, a one-pixel webbing tick between fingers. Silhouette priority: wide base, small head, rounded shoulders — avoid micro-striping; at most a two-tone limb (belly shadow / moss back).
entries.bodyPlan: Quasi-humanoid: two arms, two legs, upright stance on human leg geometry. Mild interdigital webbing to the first knuckle on hands and feet; torso a finger longer than a human baseline for wading and low bramble passage. Optional low soft ridge along the spine (connective tissue, not fin rays) — readable in large art, optional at sprite scale. No tail used for locomotion; not merfolk lower body.
entries.head: Eyes large, set slightly high, with a mild upward tilt at the outer canthus — helps with glare off water. In large art pupils may read mildly vertical-oval; at sprite scale use a dark core plus one catch-light. Ears ride close to the skull with an elongated tragus (splash shedding). Nose short with a broad bridge; nostrils angle slightly downward. Mouth wide; teeth within human range — no carnivore fangs. No horns. A low, rounded brow ridge can be present and softens with age.
entries.variation: Sex: adults share one build envelope; on average one pattern trends broader hands and feet, another trends a narrower waist and longer neck — individuals mix freely. Age: infants show webbing proportionally large relative to palm; juveniles lose facial roundness before limbs finish growing; elders show web margins slightly frayed or thinned in art, speckle contrast softened, temples a bit hollow. Regional: high-canopy lineages can read a finger longer in limb; fen-margin lineages can read slightly thicker ankles and wider feet.
entries.build: Adults are medium height with a compact core and proportionally long forearms and thighs (roughly 6.5–7.0 heads tall). Default posture: hips slightly flexed, weight mid-foot, arms ready for low branches and water edges. Children carry a larger head, shorter legs, and visibly plumper digits where webbing meets skin. Teens lengthen in limb and neck first. Elders keep the low stance but may read narrower at the waist, with more pronounced upper-back speckle fade and slightly sunken eyes.
entries.lifespan: Physical maturation about 14–16 years, prime roughly 18–40, then visible ageing with thinning web edges, softer speckle, and slightly deeper-set eyes while posture stays low. Lifespan in a typical human band; no exceptional longevity.
entries.surface: Palette: bog-green, birch-pale grey-green, wet-stone blue-grey, and brown-moss undertones. Skin often carries a fine leaf-dapple speckle or soft mottle (species pattern, not paint). Short velvety hair at crown and nape is common; body pelage is minimal. Webbing lines can carry a faint cool sheen in illustration; at 32px that collapses to one highlight pixel if needed. Lichen-toned shoulder and scapular washes are default markings, not clothing.
entries.silhouette: Wide, low stance with a rounded shoulder cap and a small head: a squat trapezoid or “reed-root” outline — reads as understory and shallow water before any face detail.
entries.Name: Skogsvattar
entries.pageBody:
## Mood and visual references

Mood: humid understory, still pools, light through leaves on water. Visual anchors: salamander and newt skin texture language on a human facial plane — not a monster muzzle, not fish-folk hybrid. Fictional race for the Matlu multiworld. Settlement data maps the old thicket-dwellers culture to Lövfolk weights; this page keeps the legacy skogsvattar id as the dedicated visual spec for sprites and codex.

Clothes, kit, and architecture are culture. Do not use this page to lock costume — only bare anatomy and default surface variation.

entries.senses: Vision biased toward motion and long sightlines under bright sky; glare tolerance useful. Hearing: human pinnae, strong lateral localisation. Smell broadly human — not bloodhound-grade. Low-frequency vibration through hooves and long bones aids awareness of herd and ground tremor at a mundane, anatomical level (no extrasensory claim).
entries.anatomy: Broad muscular waist ties human spine to equine withers; rib cage spans both segments for endurance. Long sloping shoulder into elastic pasterns; hard hooves; full equine tail. No wings, no extra limbs, no serpentine tail. Heat-adapted: large nasal turbinates implied by slightly tall nasal bridge and wide nares for dust and sustained exertion (anatomical, not magical).
entries.spriteNote: At 32px, sell centaur first: a short horizontal bar or W of legs under a 3–4px torso block; head 2px with one ear or mane slash; one-pixel tail whip off the rear. Continuous mane stroke from occiput down the back is the fastest species read. If palette is tight, merge four hooves into two dark strokes and rely on torso-over-quad overlap. No micro-face — silhouette and mane line carry identity.
entries.bodyPlan: Non-humanoid: centauroid six-limb layout — humanoid torso, arms, and head on a broad waist atop a rangy ungulate lower body (four hooved legs, barrel, tail). Matches Mistheim Viddfolk canon (open-steppe centaurs). Legacy id steppevarg labels the demographic historically tied to steppe-camp settlements before Peoples-name consolidation; it is anatomy only, not a culture.
entries.head: Human facial plan on the torso (no secondary equine head): eyes set for horizon scanning, slightly wide-set; ears human but a touch taller and more mobile than baseline, good for wind and distant hoof-fall. Nose and jaw within human range; teeth human occlusion — no enlarged canines or lupine muzzle. Hair at the human nape flows into the equine crest mane as one continuous strip — the main silhouette join cue.
entries.variation: Sex: adults share one build envelope; on average one pattern runs heavier neck and crest under the torso, another slightly wider spring of rib, with full overlap. Age: foals — long legs, small barrel, big head, no adult mane length; yearlings — awkward limb length; adults — filled chest and haunch; seniors — silvering mane/tail and human temple grey, thinner mane, possible dip behind withers. Regional: open-steppe lineages read paler and leggier; sheltered-grass variants may show a touch more fetlock hair — soft gradients for sprites, not hard sub-races.
entries.build: Adults are lean and leggy: humanoid torso within normal human proportions, joined to dry muscle over long cannons and upright pasterns; withers sit near or slightly above human shoulder height when square. Foals and children show oversized head on a small barrel, short shanks, and a higher, shorter torso — clearly juvenile. Elders hold posture but the topline may soften; cheeks hollow slightly, tail root thins; mane and tail bleach or silver while the human face shows weather lines and deeper folds — there is no separate equine head to muzzle-grey.
entries.lifespan: Human-adjacent schedule: torso and legs near adult proportions by about 16–18 years; prime from roughly 20–45; ageing shows silvering mane and tail on the equine body, leaner haunch, more pronounced wither, and lined human skin on the torso. Typical span into the 60s–70s; no exceptional longevity.
entries.surface: Equine half: short coat in dusty buckskin, clay tan, pale dune, or cool grey with optional black points; countershaded belly; dorsal stripe or shoulder ghost-marking allowed as species pigment only. Human skin on the torso where exposed: wind- and sun-roughened tan to ruddy brown. Mane and tail are coarse brush, often sun-bleached at tips; fetlock feather absent or minimal for this lineage. No scaled or armoured integument.
entries.silhouette: Long, low equine base with a compact upright torso riding above the withers — one diagonal from hoof-line to crown when moving; mane or crest stroke plus a thin head wedge reads “steppe runner” at sprite scale before any face detail.
entries.Name: Steppevarg
entries.pageBody:
## Mood and visual references

Mood: heat shimmer, long horizon, dust under hoof. Visual anchors: classical centaur read with steppe-pony conformation — rangy, drought-tough, route-runner — not wolf-headed hybrids or full-fantasy armour plates. Fictional anatomy for the Matlu multiworld; aligns to WORLD.md Viddfolk (centaurs of the open steppe). Legacy id steppevarg maps to Viddfolk in settlement data.

Clothes, route-weaves, kit, and architecture are culture. This page locks bare species surface and body plan only.

entries.senses: Vision favours detail and parallax in leaf-dappled light; bright open sky can read as harsh until eyes adjust. Hearing is strong in bands useful for wind in canopy, rope tension, and close footfalls on wood; long ears aid lateral localization. Smell is modestly above human baseline for bark resin, leaf mold, and rain on loam—shown as expressive nostril flare, never a snout.
entries.anatomy: Hands and feet are long relative to the torso, with slightly spatulate distal digits for bark and rope. Shoulder and hip mobility favours reaching, twisting, and bracing on narrow supports without needing a wide squat. Torso reads slim at low resolution because the rib cage is shallow compared to stockier peoples.
entries.spriteNote: At 32px, sell the race with one shallow crown notch or two ear pixels above the hair, a waist one pixel narrower than a human hero of matching tile height, and eyes as a bright pixel pair. Merge fingers unless a special pose needs them; the nose is optional (single midline dot at most). Feet can imply soft footwear or bare soles—that is culture in art, not a species split.
entries.bodyPlan: Quasi-humanoid: two arms, two legs, upright biped with a gracile human baseline. Limbs and digits are elongated for reach and grip in canopy environments; no wings, tail, or extra paired appendages.
entries.head: Eyes are large, forward-set, with a single clean corneal highlight; pupils round like a human’s. Ears are long, taper to a point, and rise visibly above the hairline in side view—the main silhouette marker at 32px. Nose straight and narrow with subtle nostril flare under effort. Jawline refined; teeth match human dental layout without enlarged canines. No horns or bony crests.
entries.variation: Sex: adult silhouettes overlap heavily; on average one pattern leans to squarer shoulders and jaw, another to slightly wider hips and a softer jawline—never a different body plan. Age: children show a bigger head, shorter torso, and ears that eat more vertical pixels; teens gain leg length first; elders show fine creasing like creased leaf tissue, subtle hollows at the eyes, and silver or grey streaks at the temples that can be a one-pixel highlight shift. Regional: deep-grove lineages may pick up cooler green undertones; edge-forest lineages warmer bronze—soft gradients only, not separate “subraces” for mechanics.
entries.build: Adults run about 7–8 heads tall: long femurs and forearms, shallow rib cage, narrow hips, weight often on the forefoot as if the next step might be along a line rather than flat ground. Children are still leggy but with a larger head and ears that dominate the crown at sprite scale; adolescents stretch in the legs before the shoulders widen. Elders keep much of their height but may read slightly more slender in the limbs with a gentle forward cant from the mid-back (lifelong rope and branch work, not illness).
entries.lifespan: Physical “young adult” presentation aligns to human late teens–twenties but may persist across centuries of calendar age; ageing first appears as skin texture, pigment loss in hair, and slight retraction of soft tissue rather than height collapse. Very old individuals may show a softer ear-tip line, more pronounced fine lines at the eyes, and hands that read more sinew than cushion.
entries.surface: Warm, matte skin from birch-pale through muted leaf-green undertone to sun-flecked bronze in high-contrast light. No scales, heavy pelt, or plating—only fine short vellus on forearms and shins. Optional soft dapple or vein-like mottling across shoulders or upper back reads as species pattern, not cosmetics.
entries.silhouette: A tall narrow vertical with a shallow “V” or horn-notch at the crown from ear tips—reads as “reed in the wind” before any facial pixels.
entries.Name: Sylphari
entries.pageBody:
Mood: light-boned, patient, forest-adapted humanoid—elf-line without wings, muzzle, or fey glamour tells. This row is the legacy settlement-generator slug sylphari; canon People name in WORLD.md / AXES.md is Lövfolk (eastern canopy). Use this page for anatomy and sprite reads only—costume, architecture, and culture live elsewhere.

References: WORLD.md — Lövfolk, elves of the eastern canopy (slow ageing, Grenmark, living archives). docs/peoples-and-races.md maps sylphari → Lövfolk for downstream tooling.

entries.senses: Eyes favour twilight: pupils can look large in dim light. Hearing leans to low rumbles, footfalls, and shifting stone. Nostrils are wide for damp earth, rot, and weather—not a separate animal snout. No heat pits or echolocation.
entries.anatomy: Mass is carried in the upper back, chest, and thighs; the waist is shallow at rest. Hands have thick, shorter fingers and a strong crushing grip. Feet are broad and splayed. The rib cage is laterally very wide; turns are slow. Differences from smaller humanoid races are mass, bone thickness, and weight distribution—not a new body plan.
entries.spriteNote: At 32px: prioritize torso width (often 3–4 px) and a small head (about 2×2 px) sitting low, two eye highlights or a single lit band, one heavy brow stroke, and thick legs with no fine ankle. Tusks, if used, are a 1px jaw line, not individual teeth. Crease-lichen = one midtone side pixel or skip for clarity.
entries.bodyPlan: Quasi-humanoid: two arms, two legs, full upright stance, with torso depth, limb girth, and hand/foot size far above human baselines. Five toes; no extra limbs. Proportions are not “a scaled-up human“—shorter neck, wider rib cage, and a lower center of mass than a human of similar height.
entries.head: Skull is broad and low, with a heavy brow over small, deep-set eyes (two bright points read at 32px). Nose is wide, nostrils large. Ears are small, tight to the head or mildly pointed in some lineages—never long elf ears. The mandible is heavy; canines can be prominent, and some adults show uneven, worn tusks as keratin at the tooth line, not long sabre fangs. No true horn cores: any “horn” read should be a keratin nub or knotted scar mass, not a clean spiral.
entries.variation: Sex: adults share the same overall silhouette; on average, one pattern leans to heavier jaw and deltoids, another to a slightly wider pelvis, with full overlap. Age: children read as short and broad with a big head; teens lengthen first then thicken; elders show more fissuring, jowl sag, and optional belly mass while the shoulder line stays wide. Regional (art-direction sublabels, not culture names): highland trolls can read paler with more “lichen” speckle; coastal trolls lean greener-grey with salt-weathering at the brows; inland wold trolls can pick up warmer earth undertones.
entries.build: Adult height spans a wide band by region; proportions sit around 4.5–5.5 heads tall (stocky) with a short, thick neck, deep chest, and column legs. Idle posture is a slight forward lean from the hips, bracing weight through the full foot, not a sickly stoop. Adolescents are already broad but not full height, with limbs lengthening before the chest finishes thickening. Children are short and wide, with a relatively larger head, rounder belly, and short shins.
entries.lifespan: Childhood and adolescence are slow compared to human norms. Prime endures a long midlife; ageing first shows as skin fissuring, loss of mottled contrast, hollows around the eyes, and a stiffer, slower gait. Elders can remain very broad; frailty is a late look.
entries.surface: Thick, hide-like skin: cool blue-grey to green-grey, mottled with lighter patches and deep crease shadows. Creases at jaw, elbow, and clavicle can carry lichen- or crack-like markings as species pattern (not paint). Coarse vellus on forearms and calves; knuckles and elbows often warmer grey-brown. Fingernails are thick, human in count and placement.
entries.silhouette: A low, wide trapezoid: massive shoulders and back tapering into thick pillar legs, with a small head sitting deep between the traps—“moving boulder” before any facial detail.
entries.Name: Troll
entries.pageBody:
Visual mood: still, geologic, of the land—Nordic troll-kin, not a small goblin or cartoon bridge-gremlin. The classic Seven Giants in Matlu are singular, territory-scale beings; this entry describes troll-kin as a people-scale anatomy that can stand near smaller folk without duplicating a named giant’s “mountain in a body” look. In settlement data the legacy id `troll` maps to the canonical Giants People (see `docs/peoples-and-races.md`); do not use clothes, tools, or architecture here.

Art direction references (not 1:1 copies): Scandinavian folklore trolls, hill- and stone-giant silhouettes, weathered bedrock and coastal spray-stone. Keep palette cool and mottled; avoid neon greens unless corruption is the subject of a different brief.

entries.senses: Vision prioritises distance, motion, and high-glare open sky. Night vision is not a specialty. Hearing favours low-frequency wind and distant wing-thrum; the pinna shape still supports lateral localisation. Smell is broadly human. Wide nostrils serve dust and rapid breathing at sustained exertion, not super tracking.
entries.anatomy: Beyond baseline human: (1) low patagial skin lines on the flanks, relaxed when idle; (2) very low subcutaneous fat, so tendons and muscle bands read in large art, but collapse to two or three values at 32px; (3) chest slightly wide for O₂ at altitude; (4) long toes and a useful hallux grip for ledges. No true powered flight. These traits align to high exposure (steppe, cliff) without redefining the silhouette to a new body plan.
entries.spriteNote: At 32px, carry identity with a two-pixel-tall head, one-bright eye highlight, one strong crest or plume stroke, a bunched shoulder line, and long legs (three- to four-pixel shanks). Flank patagial can be a single mid-tone line along the body silhouette; if the palette is tight, drop it and let crest + long legs do the work. No micro-face detail: silhouette first.
entries.bodyPlan: Quasi-humanoid: two arms, two legs, and an upright stance matching a human baseline, but with elongated segments, slightly long feet (tarsal emphasis, not hooves), and optional low patagial skin ribbons along the flanks that never form a true wing. Enough to read as “wind-borne” without avian muzzle or beak, and not fey or draconic.
entries.head: Eyes are large, a bit more lateral than a human’s, with room for a thin highlighted band under the cornea to suggest a nictitating sheen against bright sky and dust. Ears are medium, mildly pointed, with a forward ridge that flows into the crest. Nose is narrow, nostrils wide and mobile. Jaw tapers; teeth are within human class — do not add predator fangs. No horns. The crest is keratinous-feather, not bony prongs.
entries.variation: Sex: adults share the same build envelope; on average, one pattern runs broader shoulders and a slightly fuller crest, another runs a longer leg line, but any adult can show any mix. Age: children have large heads, short legs, and no adult crest (only a downy tuft). Teens stretch in the limbs first; the crest plumes in late adolescence. Regional: eyrie lineages can read a finger stockier and paler; open-plains lineages can read longer-legged and tanner. These are soft gradients for sprite variants, not sub-race splits.
entries.build: Adults are tall and lean (about 7.0–7.5 heads tall) with a low body mass: wire, not brawler. Slightly forward-rolled but level shoulders, long neck, weight carried on the forefoot as if the ground is a temporary stop. Adolescents are awkwardly long in limb before the chest fills. Elders keep posture upright; the plume/crest thins, hands and face show more skin desiccation, shanks can read a touch thinner in sprite as “stick” legs.
entries.lifespan: Physical maturation about 16–18 years, prime from roughly 20–45, then clear ageing with crest thinning, weathering of hands/face, and a slight “wind-posture” forward lean that is muscular habit, not kyphosis. No extraordinary lifespan; the elderly are still mobile in favourable climates, but the sprite can communicate age with a smaller plume and a narrower stance.
entries.surface: Palette leans cool: pale storm-grey, desaturated blue, sanded brown, and gull-off-white, with a wind-burn ruddiness on cheekbones, knuckles, and the bridge of the nose. Short, dense vellus on forearms, calves, and flanks, tufting into a longer plume/crest at the back of the skull and upper nape. A simple dorsal fade (slate on grey) is common; it is a species pattern, not clothing or body paint. No full pelts.
entries.silhouette: Tall, narrow vertical wedge: a small high head, wide shoulders, long legs, and a single back-swept crest or plume on the occiput — the brace that reads as “cliff-wind” at sprite scale before any face detail does.
entries.Name: Vindfarare
entries.pageBody:
## Mood and visual references

Mood: bracing, spare, high-sun and high-wind. Visual anchors: sighthound and alpine climber, not angel or gryphon — no feathered muzzle, no second pair of limbs. Fictional race for the Matlu multiworld. Settlement data now samples Viddfolk and Bergfolk mixes for the old windfarer-eyrie culture; this page is the dedicated visual spec tied to the legacy vindfarare id for sprites and the codex.

Clothes, kit, and architecture are culture. Do not use this page to lock costume — only bare anatomy and default surface variation.
